stem: A researcher tracked the number of social media followers (in thousands) for a new brand over time. The line of best fit for a scatterplot of the data is $y = 2.4x + 18$, where $x$ is the number of months since the brand launched and $y$ is the predicted number of followers in thousands. What does the value $18$ represent in this context?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The brand gained 18,000 followers each month.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The brand had a predicted 18,000 followers at the time of launch.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The brand reached 18,000 followers after 2.4 months.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The brand had a predicted 2,400 followers at the time of launch.

explanation:
In the equation $y = 2.4x + 18$, the value $18$ is the y-intercept. The y-intercept is the value of $y$ when $x = 0$.

Since $x$ represents the number of months since launch, $x = 0$ corresponds to the time of launch. Substituting $x = 0$:
$$y = 2.4(0) + 18 = 18$$

Because $y$ is measured in thousands of followers, the predicted number of followers at launch is $18 \times 1{,}000 = 18{,}000$ followers.

The value 18 represents the predicted number of followers (in thousands) when the brand launched.

distractorRationale.A: This confuses the y-intercept (18) with the slope. The slope is $2.4$, which represents the monthly change in followers (in thousands), not 18.
distractorRationale.B: This is the correct answer. The y-intercept ($x = 0$, the launch date) gives $y = 18$ thousand followers.
distractorRationale.C: This confuses the roles of slope and y-intercept. The value 2.4 is the slope (monthly rate of change), not the number of months it takes to reach 18,000 followers. Setting $y = 18$ and solving $18 = 2.4x + 18$ gives $x = 0$, not $x = 2.4$.
distractorRationale.D: This reads the slope value ($2.4$ thousand) as the y-intercept, misidentifying which coefficient represents the starting value.
